(iff (append x y) (or (consp x) y)))

(equal (nth (nfix n) x) (nth n x))) "

Note: Matt Kaufmann reported that the following lemma got expensive in one of his books, so we now disable it by default and instead leave enabled a -cheap rule with a backchain limit.

"

(implies (<= (len x) (nfix n)) (equal (nth n x) nil)) :rule-classes ((:rewrite :backchain-limit-lst 1))) "

Lemmas about @(see acl2-count) of nth

"
;; Added by Alessandro Coglio (coglio@kestrel.edu), Kestrel Institute. (implies (consp x) (< (acl2-count (nth i x)) (acl2-count x))) :rule-classes :linear)
;; Added by Alessandro Coglio (coglio@kestrel.edu), Kestrel Institute. (<= (acl2-count (nth i x)) (acl2-count x)) :rule-classes :linear)

;; Added by Alessandro Coglio (coglio@kestrel.edu), Kestrel Institute. (equal (< (acl2-count (nth i x)) (acl2-count x)) (or (consp x) (> (acl2-count x) 0)))) "

Nth with other basic list functions

Note that we don't prove a rule about @(see update-nth), because @('nth-update-nth') is an AC
(implies (< (nfix n) (len x)) (member (nth n x) x)))
(equal (nth n (append x y)) (if (< (nfix n) (len x)) (nth n x) (nth (- n (len x)) y))))
(equal (nth n (revappend x y)) (if (< (nfix n) (len x)) (nth (- (len x) (+ 1 (nfix n))) x) (nth (- n (len x)) y))))
(equal (nth n (rev x)) (if (< (nfix n) (len x)) (nth (- (len x) (+ 1 (nfix n))) x) nil)))
(equal (nth n (reverse x)) (if (< (nfix n) (len x)) (nth (- (len x) (+ 1 (nfix n))) x) nil)))
(equal (nth i (take n l)) (if (< (nfix i) (nfix n)) (nth i l) nil)))
(equal (nth n (make-list-ac m val acc)) (if (< (nfix n) (nfix m)) val (nth (- n (nfix m)) acc)))) (encapsulate () (local (defun my-induct (n m) (if (zp m) (list n m) (my-induct (- n 1) (- m 1)))))
(equal (nth n (repeat m a)) (if (< (nfix n) (nfix m)) a nil)) :hints(("Goal" :induct (my-induct n m) :in-theory (enable repeat)))))
(equal (nth n (nthcdr m x)) (nth (+ (nfix n) (nfix m)) x)))
(equal (nth n (last x)) (if (zp n) (car (last x)) nil)))

(equal (nth n (butlast x m)) (if (< (nfix n) (- (len x) (nfix m))) (nth n x) nil))) "
